title: bupleurum petraeum subvar. incurvum
intro: Bupleurum petraeum subvar. incurvum, also known as rock hemlock or dwarf hare's ear, is a perennial plant species in the family Apiaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The plant has been reported to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and supporting immune function. Bupleurum petraeum subvar. incurvum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
